cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to find the best title for this passage about body language in the office. The passage starts by talking about how important body language is, saying it communicates a lot more than words. Then it lists some things not to do in the office, like touching co-workers, avoiding eye contact, having a bad handshake, and leaning your head while listening. The options are A, B, C, D. Let's break them down. Option A: Pay attention to your body language at the office. That seems to fit because the whole passage is about what not to do with your body language in the office setting. It gives specific tips to avoid mistakes, so the main idea is about being careful with your body language there. Option B: How to get along with your co-workers. While the passage does mention interactions with co-workers, the focus is more on body language specifically, not general ways to get along. So maybe B is too broad. Option C: The meaning of body language you should know. This is a bit vague. The passage isn't just explaining the meanings of body language in general, but specifically the mistakes to avoid in an office context. So C might not be as precise. Option D: Different countries have different body language. The passage doesn't mention cultural differences at all. It's all about general office behavior, so D is probably not the right choice. So the best answer is
